title: Comment on Trump Budget Numbers Released Thursday, March 16, 2017
summary: If this budget or anything close to it were enacted into law, the result would be a severe setback for international relations and America's standing in the world; for clean air and water for Americans: for progress in conquering cancer, influenza, and Alzheimer's; for the fight against human-caused climate change; for U.S. leadership in science and technology; for culture and the arts; and, yes, for American business, which depends on government services far more heavily than is generally recognized. It can only be hoped that this misguided budget will be resoundingly rejected by Congress on a bipartisan basis. John P. Holdren, Ph.D. Teresa and John Heinz Professor of Environmental Policy, Harvard Kennedy School of Government Senior Advisor to the President, The Woods Hole Research Center Formerly President Obama’s Science Advisor and Director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (January 2009 – January 2017)
body: If this budget or anything close to it were enacted into law, the result would be a severe setback for international relations and America's standing in the world; for clean air and water for Americans: for progress in conquering cancer, influenza, and Alzheimer's; for the fight against human-caused climate change; for U.S. leadership in science and technology; for culture and the arts; and, yes, for American business, which depends on government services far more heavily than is generally recognized. It can only be hoped that this misguided budget will be resoundingly rejected by Congress on a bipartisan basis. John P. Holdren, Ph.D. Teresa and John Heinz Professor of Environmental Policy, Harvard Kennedy School of Government Senior Advisor to the President, The Woods Hole Research Center Formerly President Obama’s Science Advisor and Director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (January 2009 – January 2017) The latest from Belfer See more Statements and views expressed in this commentary are solely those of the author and do not imply endorsement by Harvard University, the Harvard Kennedy School, or the Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs. Recommended citation Holdren, John P. "Comment on Trump Budget Numbers Released Thursday, March 16, 2017." _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article presents a critical commentary on the proposed Trump administration budget released on March 16, 2017. It argues that if enacted, the budget would cause severe damage across multiple critical areas. These include undermining international relations and America's global standing, harming environmental protections for clean air and water, and stalling medical progress against diseases like cancer and Alzheimer's. Furthermore, it would negatively impact the fight against human-caused climate change, diminish U.S. leadership in science and technology, harm culture and the arts, and ultimately hurt American businesses that rely on government services. The author, a former presidential science advisor, expresses hope that Congress will reject the budget on a bipartisan basis.
result.main_content_zh: 本文对2017年3月16日发布的特朗普政府预算提案提出了严厉批评。评论认为，如果该预算或类似版本成为法律，将在多个关键领域造成严重挫折。这包括损害国际关系和美国的全球地位，破坏清洁空气和水的环境保护，阻碍对抗癌症和阿尔茨海默病等疾病的医学进展。此外，预算还将对应对人为气候变化、美国在科技领域的领导力、文化艺术以及依赖政府服务的美国企业产生负面影响。作者作为前总统科学顾问，希望国会能以两党合作的方式坚决否决这一被误导的预算案。
